title: Binary search tree operations deterministic concept bank

publication.changeSummary: Added four pinned-runtime executable parity drafts for Binary search tree operations; all remain blocked from formal exams pending independent human review.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted DSA draft awaiting independent algorithm and four-language implementation review.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewers must verify concept correctness, language parity, equivalence, and grading evidence before formal use.
items.title: Binary search tree operations: reject the invalid invariant
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this DSA misconception? Every binary tree supports logarithmic search by comparing keys.
hints: Compare both options with the shared invariant, counterexample, and complexity boundary.

feedback.correct: Correct. Only a maintained search-order invariant enables ordered search, and complexity is O(height), not automatically logarithmic.
feedback.incorrect: That preserves the misconception. Only a maintained search-order invariant enables ordered search, and complexity is O(height), not automatically logarithmic.

criteria.description: Selects the source-linked algorithmic correction rather than the misconception.

options.text: Only a maintained search-order invariant enables ordered search, and complexity is O(height), not automatically logarithmic.
Every binary tree supports logarithmic search by comparing keys.

answer.explanation: Only a maintained search-order invariant enables ordered search, and complexity is O(height), not automatically logarithmic.

items.title: Binary search tree operations: C parity contract
items.prompt: Read the same tree encoding and print whether it satisfies strict binary-search-tree bounds. This is the C variant of one language-neutral input/output contract for Binary search tree operations.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted four-language DSA parity draft; independent algorithm, language-idiom, test-oracle, safety, pedagogy, and accessibility review is required.

hints: Preserve the exact input/output contract and handle the visible boundary without relying on hidden values.

feedback.correct: The visible and hidden deterministic parity checks passed.
feedback.incorrect: At least one contract check failed; inspect the invariant, boundary, and exact output without revealing hidden data.

criteria.description: Passes both equivalent normal and boundary contracts in the pinned language runtime.
